name: W. E. B. Du Bois
abstract: William Edward Burghardt Du Bois ( doo-BOYSS; February 23, 1868 – August 27, 1963) was an American sociologist, writer, historian, and Pan-Africanist civil rights activist. Born in Great Barrington, Massachusetts, Du Bois grew up in a relatively tolerant and integrated community.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Rollins
about_quotes.evidence: I remember we would be playing ball and stuff, and I remember W.E.B. Du Bois, you know, he would be coming home every night, I’d see him coming home... he’d sort of look at us
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Rollins — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: my mother was a member of the Abyssinian and she belonged to the Negro History Club, uh, Association and we were, they were always entertaining people like W.E.B. DuBois.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
